data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: MVST, RKLB, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday morning as investors awaited the release of both the Jobs Report and Consumer Sentiment data later in the day. These key economic indicators are closely watched, as the jobs report provides insight into employment trends, while consumer sentiment offers a gauge of consumer confidence and spending patterns, both of which influence market expectations for economic growth and potential policy decisions.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Shares of Microvast Holdings (NASDAQ:MVST) surged 44% following the company's announcement of a major milestone in the development of its True All-Solid-State Battery (ASSB) technology. This breakthrough represents a significant advancement in battery safety, energy density, and efficiency, offering solutions for applications like data center backup power systems and electric school buses while also paving the way for innovations in electric vehicles and robotics. Unlike conventional lithium-ion or semi-solid-state batteries, Microvast's ASSB eliminates liquid electrolytes and features a bipolar stacking architecture, which allows higher voltages unattainable by traditional battery designs. As Microvast progresses to pilot production, the company aims to revolutionize the battery industry with scalable, customizable energy storage solutions for high-demand sectors.
  • Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares rose more than 3% after the company inked an agreement with NASA to provide Neutron launch services through its existing VADR (Venture-Class Acquisition of Dedicated and Rideshare) contract. Neutron, Rocket Lab's new medium-lift reusable rocket, will expand the company's ability to deliver a wide range of missions, including CubeSats, Class D missions, and other payloads, across various orbits. Additionally, the Neutron rocket is positioned to be integrated into the U.S. Government’s National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Lane 1 program, a $5.6B IDIQ contract. Neutron is scheduled to debut with its first launch from Rocket Lab's Launch Complex 3 in Virginia in mid-2025.
  • Formerly known as Smart Global Holdings, Penguin Solutions (NASDAQ:PENG) shares soared 17% after the company revealed a strategic collaboration agreement with SK Telecom and SK hynix to develop and deliver comprehensive AI data center solutions. The agreement, signed at CES 2025 in Las Vegas, follows SKT's $200M strategic investment in Penguin Solutions, which was completed in December. This follows the company's upbeat FQ1 results.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor's Q4 revenue beats estimates amid growing AI demand
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's (NYSE:TSM) revenue for December 2024 surged 57.8% year-over-year to about NT$278.16B, amid a growing demand for AI applications.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) — saw revenue for the fourth quarter of 2024 grow about 39% year-over-year to around NT$868.46B. (The Q4 figures were based on calculations off monthly disclosures — October NT$314.24B, November NT$276.06B, December NT$278.16B).

The company's fourth quarter revenue of NT$868.46B, beat analysts' average estimate of NT$854.7B, according to a report from Bloomberg.

TSM's revenue for the full year 2024 jumped 33.9% to NT$2.894T, compared to 2023 when it generated NT$2.161T.

The Taiwan-based company's revenue for December 2024 rose 0.8% compared to November 2024.

The strong numbers from TSM strengthen expectations that big tech companies from Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) to Microsoft (MSFT) will continue to build and upgrade datacenters to boost AI development, the report added.

Earlier this week, Microsoft said it would invest $3B in India for cloud and AI infrastructure over the next two years. The tech giant has already unveiled its larger plan to spend $80B in data centers in fiscal 2025.

Over the past weekend, AI server maker Foxconn Technology —which is formally known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) (OTCPK:HNHPF) — reported 15.17% year-over-year growth in fourth quarter revenue to NT$2.132T, setting a record for the company's highest fourth quarter revenue ever.

TSM is slated to report its fourth quarter results on Jan. 16.

Shares of TSM rose about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Rupert Murdoch's Fox Corp. faces $2.7 billion lawsuit over Smartmatic defamation claim
data.description:
A New York state appeals court ruled on Thursday that Fox Corporation (NASDAQ:FOX) (NASDAQ:FOXA), parent of Fox News Network, must face a $2.7 billion defamation lawsuit filed by Smartmatic—a UK based voting machinery company.

Lawsuit background: Smartmatic initiated a lawsuit in February 2021, claiming that the media giant spread false information about Smartmatic's involvement in election fraud during the 2020 election. The lawsuit alleges that the parent directed on-air talent that was repeatedly played on Fox News, calling it a "disinformation campaign" to regain viewership after Joe Biden's victory.

"Fox News Network's daily operations in connection with that coverage sufficiently allege Fox Corporation's "direct liability" for the challenged defamatory statements, as well as satisfy the applicable pleading requirements for alleging actual malice," the court said.

“We will be ready to defend this case surrounding extremely newsworthy events when it goes to trial,” Fox commented, according to a Reuters report. “As a report prepared by our financial expert shows, Smartmatic’s damages claims are implausible, disconnected from reality, and on their face intended to chill First Amendment freedoms.”

Previously, in 2023, Fox Corp. (FOX) paid more than $787 million to settle that lawsuit brought by Dominion Voting Systems.

data.title: Dalata sells Clayton Whites Hotel Wexford to Neville for €21M
data.description:
• Dalata Hotel Group (OTCPK:DLTTF) announced on Friday the sale of Clayton Whites Hotel Wexford to Neville Hotels for €21M.
  • DLTTF operates four-star hotels and boutique hotels, and also engages in financing, catering, hotel management, and property investment and holding activities.

data.title: European markets steady, Ubisoft's shares slip
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.06%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.15%

France (CAC:IND) +0.30%

Industrial production in France rose by 0.2% month-on-month in November.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) was little changed -0.03% on Friday, even as it was on course for a second weekly gain, as investors assessed the latest regional economic data and ongoing turbulence in U.K.’s debt markets.

Meanwhile, the British pound slumped to its lowest against the U.S. dollar in more than a year, trading at $1.2284 at 7:25 a.m. London time.

On the corporate front, Ubisoft's shares fell after the French video game maker said it had appointed strategic advisors to review its options following reports last year that a buyout was under consideration.

Coming up in the session: December U.S. nonfarm payrolls' data awaited later in the day.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.70%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 2.56%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.84%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

More on markets

  • Trump tariff fears causing disconnect in silver, copper markets - Bloomberg
  • Asian markets continue downward trend over Fed policy concerns
  • Euro stocks can offer a lesson in high concentration for the U.S. – Goldman

data.title: Free speech vs. national security: TikTok heads to Supreme Court over U.S. ban
data.description:
TikTok's last-ditch effort to block a potential U.S. ban begins Friday, when the Supreme Court will hear arguments over the constitutionality of a law requiring the divestment of the social media app from its Chinese parent ByteDance (BDNCE).

The app said it will shut down in the U.S. on January 19, as required by the law, unless the Supreme Court strikes down or delays the deadline. Congress had passed the law in April, which mandates the sale of the app to a U.S. company or face a ban due to national security concerns over its foreign ownership.

TikTok has repeatedly argued that the law violates the First Amendment rights of American creators and users. "Rarely if ever has the Court confronted a free speech case that matters to so many people," it stated in its appeal to the Supreme Court, pointing to the 170M Americans that use TikTok on a regular basis.

On the other hand, the Department of Justice said the ban is aimed at protecting American users' sensitive data and preventing covert manipulation by China. "Congress' data-protection goal has nothing to do with expression at all," it said.

President-elect Donald Trump last month requested the Supreme Court to pause the TikTok ban, which would allow his incoming administration time to "negotiate a resolution to save the platform while addressing national security concerns."

If the ban gets implemented, Wall Street analysts see Meta (META) and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) as the biggest beneficiaries. Other social media firms like Reddit (RDDT), Pinterest (PINS) and Snap (SNAP) stand to benefit as well.

data.title: BEST receives NYSE notice over delayed filing of financial information
data.description:
• China-based supply chain service provider BEST (NYSE:BEST) announced on Friday the receipt of an NYSE notice over delayed filing of its financial information.
  • The notice said BEST is not in compliance with NYSE's continued listing requirements under the timely filing criteria.
  • The company failed to timely file its Form 6-K containing semi-annual financial information for the half year ended June 30, 2024, with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • BEST attributed the delay to focusing its resources on the previously announced privatization.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Macy's to close 66 under-performing stores across U.S.
data.description:
Macy's (NYSE:M) is shuttering 66 stores, including nine in New York and nine in California, as part of its ongoing strategy to close 150 under-performing locations through 2026.

The retailer's strategy, announced in February, is aimed at returning to sustainable, profitable sales growth and upgrading 350 stores that are more profitable.

"We are closing under-productive Macy's stores to allow us to focus our resources and prioritize investments in our go-forward stores, where customers are already responding positively to better product offerings and elevated service," said Macy's (NYSE:M) CEO Tony Spring.

Under Macy's (M) 'Bold New Chapter' strategy, investments in the First 50 stores to improve customer experience have boosted sales for three straight quarters. The company plans to bring this enhanced customer experience to more locations nationwide and through its digital channels.

While the performance at the First 50 stores appears promising, Investing Group Leader Elephant Analytics said it's uncertain how much of this outperformance may be due to these stores being generally stronger.

"However, Macy's noted that some of its lower-volume stores (in the First 50) were having the biggest increases in sales," he added.

data.title: BlackRock in talks to sell stake in the entertainment company Authentic Brands - report
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) is in talks to sell its equity stake in Authentic Brands to a group of investors including CVC Capital Partners, General Atlantic, HPS Investment Partners, and Leonard Green & Partners, insiders told Bloomberg News.

The investing group is discussing the terms of the deal, which could value the stake in the entertainment company at $3B or more, Bloomberg reported on Friday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The transaction has not been finalized yet, noted the people who reportedly asked not to be identified discussing confidential information.

The U.S.'s largest asset manager is said to have become the largest investor in Authentic Brands after the BLK fund Long-Term Private Capital bought a $875M stake in August 2019.

BlackRock had recently decided to wind down the Long-Term Private Capital fund and return capital to investors, Bloomberg noted.

data.title: Apollo eyes $9.5 billion stake in Japan's Seven & i management buyout - report
data.description:
Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) is weighing a significant investment of up to ¥1.5 trillion (approximately $9.5 billion) in a management buyout proposal for Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDF), Bloomberg reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter. The move is part of an effort led by the founding Ito family to take the Japanese convenience store giant private.

Apollo (APO) could potentially become a key investor alongside the Ito family and Itochu Corp. Itochu considers over ¥1 trillion in investment, while the Ito family about ¥500 billion. Other parties are still negotiating stakes.

The current proposal calls for equity stakes to amount to roughly ¥4 trillion, with the banks providing the remaining funding, people with knowledge said. However, the proposal is subject to change.

Discussions are still ongoing regarding various aspects of the deal, including investment structure, voting rights, and board composition, the report added.

The Ito family of Japan—founders of the 7-Eleven chain—were earlier considering a ¥9 trillion management buyout, above ¥7.5 trillion takeover bid from Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (ATD:CA)—which may now be reduced as the Japanese company's market value is still well behind the offers.

Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDF) showed a market valuation of approximately ¥6.3 trillion after reporting a significant decline in profits in its Q3 results. The stock price traded ~4.9% on Friday on the Japanese exchange.

“We are considering all options, and whether they are feasible,” Seven & i’s (OTCPK:SVNDF) CFO Yoshimichi Maruyama commented, stating that the board is still considering takeover proposals by Couche-Tard (ATD:CA) and the Ito family.

“Both proposals have hurdles to make an acquisition possible. We haven’t received details of those solutions, so the ball is on the side of those proposing buyouts,” he added.

Apollo Global (APO) did not immediately reply to Seeking Alpha's request for comment on the matter.
